report.title: Institutional AGI/ASI Governance: Technical Blueprint and Master Reference (2026–2030)
report.abstract: This master reference defines the technical and strategic framework for Global Systemically Important Financial Institutions (G-SIFIs) and Global 2000 organizations to navigate the transition to Artificial General Intelligence. It harmonizes established prudential mandates (Basel III, SR 11-7) with emerging AI regulations (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF) through a unified "Trust Stack" reference architecture. Key technical implementations include production-grade Kafka ACL governance, OPA-based compliance-as-code sidecars, and WORM-based evidence storage for forensic non-repudiation.
report.content:
$(cat COMPENDIUM_STRATEGIC_PILLARS.md)
$(cat COMPENDIUM_TRUST_STACK.md)
$(cat GSIFI_TECHNICAL_IMPL.md)
$(cat COMPENDIUM_GLOBAL_GOVERNANCE.md)
# Part IV: Institutional Rollout (2026–2030) The deployment of this framework follows a phased "Intelligence Sovereignty" roadmap: 1. **Year 1: The Identity Foundation**: Rollout of SPIFFE-based agent identities and Kafka WORM logging. 2. **Year 2: Autonomous Orchestration**: Migration of high-risk financial models to the Sentinel Compliance sidecar. 3. **Year 3: Global Cooperation**: Integration with the ICGC Compute Registries and multi-lateral crisis simulations.
# Conclusion For G-SIFIs, AGI governance is not merely a compliance task but a fundamental requirement for institutional survival. By codifying governance into the infrastructure layer, we ensure that as intelligence scales, so does our capacity for deterministic control and systemic stability.
